design_rule: This is the safe default for existing 26.2 worlds. It improves host rocks and deposit height bands without overriding the dimension type.

layers.name: surface_regolith_and_weathering
layers.note: Laterite and placer deposits are biased upward. They stand in for climate and river sorting until a biome-aware generator exists.

layers.name: sedimentary_basin
layers.note: Coal, phosphate and evaporite beds should read as broad sedimentary resources rather than isolated ore pebbles.

layers.name: felsic_intrusive_and_hydrothermal_belt
layers.note: Copper, gold, mercury and beryl are grouped around felsic intrusions and hydrothermal fracture systems.

layers.name: mafic_volcanic_and_iron_belt
layers.note: Magnetite is anchored to deeper mafic or volcanic backgrounds while low-grade ore stays common enough for day-one iron.

layers.name: metamorphic_basement
layers.note: High-rank coal, lapis, redstone and rare carbonatites are kept deeper than ordinary sedimentary resources.

layers.name: mantle_derived_pipes
layers.note: Kimberlite remains rare and pipe-like; diamondiferous material is a rarer subfeature.

compatibility.vanilla_ore_features_removed_by: earth_on_minecraft:add/remove biome modifiers plus minecraft:ore_veininess noise override
compatibility.existing_chunks: Already-generated chunks may still contain old vanilla ore blocks.
compatibility.world_height: No dimension height override in the default profile.
